metadata.title: being good at ai is (stupidly) simple

beats.narration_text: this is Liam, in for Bear. The useful idea is a four-level ladder for AI fluency, and most people stop at level one. The whole thing is structured so that wherever you quit reading, you still leave with something usable. That's the design. Let's break it down.

props.segment: Being Good At AI Is (Stupidly) Simple
props.command: What are the four levels of Claude fluency, and what separates each one?

beats.narration_text: Level one is just getting started on the right model. You go to claude.ai, create an account, find the model selector at the bottom right of the chat box. The free tier gives you Sonnet. You want Opus — paid, smarter — with Thinking set to High. The point: most people never do this. They sign up, use the free model, get mediocre results, conclude AI is overrated. The problem isn't AI. It's the model they're running.

artifactLines: Go to claude.ai → create account → verify phone.
Bottom-right corner: model selector. Free = Sonnet. You want Opus.
Set Thinking to High. Turn on Thinking mode.
Then use AskUserQuestion: 'Help me do [X]. Use AskUserQuestion first.'

beats.narration_text: The important move inside level one is the move that makes you a power user: AskUserQuestion. You don't prompt Claude. You ask Claude to prompt you instead. The formula is dead simple — Help me do X for Y, use AskUserQuestion first. Claude comes back with three to five targeted questions. You answer them. Then the output is dramatically better than anything you'd get from a cold one-shot prompt. Make it part of your default prompt pattern.

shot.slotNote: SLOT — fill media/B02.png (screenshot of Claude using AskUserQuestion — showing the question interface with clickable answer options and 'Something else' text box)

beats.narration_text: Level two: talk to your computer instead of typing. You speak four times faster than you type. One practical option is Wispr Flow — free tool, AI-powered, you talk and it writes into whatever box is focused on your screen. You'd use it right inside the Claude chat box. The productivity gain is immediate. If you're still hunting and pecking your prompts one word at a time, you're leaving most of the speed advantage on the table.

artifactLines: You speak 4x faster than you type. Use that.
Wispr Flow: free, AI-powered, works inside any text box including Claude.
Talk → Wispr transcribes → Claude receives a fuller, faster prompt.
This alone moves you past most AI users.

beats.narration_text: Level three is Claude Cowork — the desktop app, not the browser. The distinction matters. Browser Claude gives you text back, and you copy-paste it somewhere. Cowork creates the actual file and saves it to your computer. The real PDF. The real spreadsheet. You point Cowork at one folder, drop in a messy document or last month's numbers, and then prompt: build me a spreadsheet to do X, but ask what you need first. Three minutes later: a clean Excel file sitting in your folder.

shot.slotNote: SLOT — fill media/B04.png (screenshot of Claude Cowork tab in desktop app with a folder selected, showing a completed spreadsheet or document output in the Cowork folder)

beats.narration_text: Level four is connecting your apps. Left sidebar inside Claude: Customize, then Connectors. Claude integrates with Gmail, Google Calendar, meeting note tools like Granola or Otter, and hundreds more. Once connected, you can ask Claude to read your email, cross-reference your calendar, pull action items from your last meeting transcript, and give you a summary — all in one prompt. No copy-pasting. No switching tabs. Claude is the hub. This is the top zero-point-something percent of AI users.

artifactLines: Customize → Connectors: Gmail, Calendar, Granola, Otter, and 200+ more.
Ask Claude to read email + calendar + meeting notes in one prompt.
No copy-paste. No tab-switching. Claude has the full context.
Test prompt: 'Read my email, my last meeting, and tell me what needs doing today.'

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Wherever you stopped on the four-level ladder, take the next level today. Paste this to find out exactly where you are and what to do next.

props.segment: Being Good At AI Is (Stupidly) Simple
props.command: I currently use AI at level [1/2/3]. What's the single highest-leverage thing I should set up next to reach the next level? Ask me clarifying questions first.

beats.narration_text: Four levels. Stupidly simple. Pick the next one.

props.title: being good at ai is (stupidly) simple
